agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific int

report.hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in TM5/ECL2 pockets, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target (SSTR3/5) affinity by avoiding conserved polar interactions in those subtypes.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current design strategy is capable of achieving high selectivity (Δmargin >

report.hypothesis: The current design strategy is capable of achieving high selectivity (Δmargin > 0), but the low pass rate indicates a need for tighter structural and energetic constraints to improve candidate quality and throughput.

proposed_changes.rationale: The current ddG threshold is too lenient, allowing candidates with weak binding (e.g., iter20_cand003 with ddG=-23.3). Tightening the ddG threshold will filter out weaker binders and improve the structural quality of selected candidates.

To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing mutations on these positions will help break off-target interactions while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14).
